sections.heading: Administration Commands
sections.body:
> **Note:** Any MongoDB user in the Atlas project with the
> [atlasAdmin](atlas/security-add-mongodb-users) role
> can retrieve and update the federated database instance configuration.

sections.heading: Stores
sections.body:
The MongoDB Shell, [mongosh](mongodb-shell), lets you create, remove, and retrieve stores in
your federated database instance storage configuration using the following commands:

- [createStore](adf-create-store-cmd)

- [stores](adf-drop-store-cmd)

- [listStores](adf-list-stores-cmd)

sections.heading: Database
sections.body:
The MongoDB Shell, [mongosh](mongodb-shell), lets you remove a database from your federated database instance storage
configuration using the following commands:

- [databases](adf-drop-database-cmd)

sections.heading: Collections and Views
sections.body:
The MongoDB Shell, [mongosh](mongodb-shell), lets you create, remove, and/or rename collections
and views in your federated database instance storage configuration using the following
commands:

- [create (Data Federation Command)](adf-create-collection-views-cmd)

- [drop (Data Federation Command)](adf-drop-collection-views-cmd)

- [renameCollection](adf-rename-collection-cmd)

sections.heading: Authentication Options
sections.body:
Data Federation uses [SCRAM-SHA](manual/core/security-scram), [self-managed X.509 certificate](self-managed-x509), [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization), and [AWS IAM Authentication](set-up-pwdless-auth) for authentication. It doesn't support
[LDAP](atlas/security-ldaps).
